title: Array Index Out of Bounds in Sum of Array

statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.

editorial.title: Array Index Out of Bounds in Sum of Array

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: int sumArray(vector<int>& nums) { return nums[0]++; } };
starterCode.java: public class Solution { public int sumArray(int[] nums) { return nums[0]++; } }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def sumArray(self, nums) -> int: return nums[0] + 1

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: int sumArray(vector<int>& nums) { return nums[0]++; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public int sumArray(int[] nums) { return nums[0]++; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def sumArray(self, nums) -> int: return nums[0] + 1

solutions.python: def sum_array(nums): n = int(input()) nums = list(map(int,input().split())) return sum(nums) if __name__ == '__main__': n = int(input()) nums = list(map(int,input().split())) print(sum_array(nums))
solutions.cpp: #include <bits/stdc++.h> using namespace std; class Solution { public: int sumArray(vector<int>& nums) { return accumulate(nums.begin(),nums.end(),0); } }; int main() { int n; cin >> n; vector<int> nums(n); for(int i = 0; i < n; i++) { cin >> nums[i]; } cout << Solution().sumArray(nums) << endl; return 0; }
